title: From/Into Conversion Pattern
description: Implement the From trait to define type conversions. Implementing From<T> for U automatically gives you Into<U> for T. This pattern is used pervasively in Rust for ergonomic APIs, error conversion with the ? operator, and flexible function signatures using impl Into<T>.
whenToUse: Converting between your types and standard types
Accepting flexible input in function signatures: impl Into<String>
Parsing or transforming data between representations
realWorldUse: String::from(&str), PathBuf::from(String), IpAddr::from([127,0,0,1]), every error type with #[from] in thiserror. The ? operator relies on From to convert error types.
